title: January-February Asteroid Campaign Results
summary: Asteonetica volunteers in Kenya completed a concentrated asteroid review campaign using IASC survey data and internal verification workflows.
content:
The January-February observing cycle marked one of the clearest coordinated asteroid review efforts yet for the Kenya-based Asteroid Afronauts team. Across the campaign window, volunteers worked through sequential image sets, flagged motion candidates, and documented frame-by-frame reasoning for each preliminary asteroid signature.

The campaign emphasized disciplined review rather than speed alone. Participants compared suspected motion against known-object behaviour, ruled out grid flickering and brightness fluctuation artifacts, and refined internal reporting habits before submission.

For Asteonetica, dispatch records like this are part campaign note, part research memory. They allow Kenya asteroid work to be documented in a way that is readable both to future volunteers and to the wider scientific community.
